ccpa_maximum.name: CCPA/CPRA Maximum Pressure
ccpa_maximum.applicable_when: Broker does business in California OR processes CA residents' data
ccpa_maximum.subject: CCPA/CPRA Data Deletion Request, Right to Know, and Withdrawal of Consent — {full_name}
ccpa_maximum.body:
To the Privacy Officer or Data Protection Contact:

I am exercising my rights under the California Consumer Privacy Act
(CCPA) as amended by the California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA),
Cal. Civ. Code §§ 1798.100–1798.199.100.

This communication constitutes THREE separate requests:

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
REQUEST 1: RIGHT TO DELETE (Cal. Civ. Code § 1798.105)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

I request that you delete ALL personal information you have collected
about me, including but not limited to:

— Full name and any aliases or name variations
— Current and historical physical addresses
— Email addresses
— Phone numbers
— Date of birth and age
— Employment history
— Education history
— Names of relatives and associates
— Social media profiles
— Photographs
— Court and public records
— Financial information and income estimates
— Political affiliations and inferences
— Any other personal information as defined by Cal. Civ. Code § 1798.140(v)

I further request that you direct any service providers or third parties
to whom you have disclosed my personal information to delete it as well,
per Cal. Civ. Code § 1798.105(c).

If your search identifies multiple entries that could reasonably
pertain to me — including partial matches, variant spellings, or
records linked to different addresses or identifiers — I exercise
my right to know (§ 1798.110) to review those entries so I can
confirm that every record pertaining to me is deleted. Do not
unilaterally determine which records are or are not mine without
providing me the opportunity to verify.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
REQUEST 2: RIGHT TO KNOW (Cal. Civ. Code § 1798.110)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

I request that you disclose:

a) The categories of personal information you have collected about me
b) The categories of sources from which my personal information was collected
c) The business or commercial purpose for collecting my personal information
d) The categories of third parties to whom you have disclosed, sold, or
   shared my personal information
e) The specific pieces of personal information you have collected about me

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
REQUEST 3: OPT-OUT & WITHDRAWAL OF CONSENT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Pursuant to Cal. Civ. Code § 1798.120:

I opt out of the sale of my personal information.
I opt out of the sharing of my personal information for cross-context
behavioral advertising.

Additionally, I EXPLICITLY WITHDRAW ANY AND ALL CONSENT — express or
implied, past, present, or future — for your organization to:

— Collect my personal information from any source
— Process my personal information for any purpose
— Sell, share, license, or otherwise disclose my personal information
  to any third party
— Create, maintain, or update any profile or record about me
— Use my personal information for any inference, derivation, or profiling

This withdrawal of consent is unconditional, irrevocable, and applies
in perpetuity. I do not consent to any future collection, processing,
sale, or sharing of my personal information by your organization,
regardless of the source from which you may obtain it.

NOTE: I understand that withdrawal of consent may not be legally
binding in all jurisdictions at this time. I am establishing a clear,
documented record of my wishes. Any future processing of my personal
information by your organization will occur AGAINST my explicitly
stated objection, which may be relevant under current or future
privacy legislation.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
IDENTIFYING INFORMATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Full Name: {full_name}
Email: {email}
Phone: {phone}
Address: {address}
Date of Birth: {dob}

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Under the CCPA/CPRA, you are required to:
— Respond to this request within 45 calendar days
— Confirm the specific personal information deleted
— Identify all third parties notified of the deletion
— Provide the disclosures requested in Request 2

Failure to comply may result in enforcement action by the California
Privacy Protection Agency and/or civil penalties of up to $7,500 per
intentional violation (Cal. Civ. Code § 1798.155).

This request is made under all privacy laws applicable to your
organization based on your place of incorporation, headquarters,
registration, and operations — in addition to those applicable
based on my residence. Similar obligations may arise under the
GDPR (if you are established in or target the EU/UK), the
California Delete Act, and the privacy laws of every state and
country in which you operate.

This communication is being archived with timestamp verification
for potential future legal reference.

Sincerely,
{full_name}
Date: {date}

gdpr_maximum.name: GDPR Maximum Pressure
gdpr_maximum.applicable_when: Broker processes EU/UK resident data or offers services in EU/UK
gdpr_maximum.subject: GDPR Articles 15, 17, and 21 — Data Subject Rights Request — {full_name}
gdpr_maximum.body:
To the Data Protection Officer:

I am exercising my rights under the General Data Protection Regulation
(EU) 2016/679 ("GDPR") and, where applicable, the UK GDPR.

This communication constitutes the following requests:

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
1. RIGHT OF ACCESS (Article 15)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

I request a copy of all personal data you hold about me, including:
— The purposes of processing
— The categories of personal data concerned
— The recipients to whom my data has been disclosed
— The envisaged retention period
— The source of the data (where not collected directly from me)
— The existence of automated decision-making, including profiling
— Where data is transferred to a third country, the safeguards applied

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
2. RIGHT TO ERASURE (Article 17)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

I request the erasure of all personal data you hold about me.
The grounds for this request include:
— The data is no longer necessary for the purpose for which it was collected
— I withdraw my consent (Article 6(1)(a))
— I object to the processing (Article 21) and there are no overriding
  legitimate grounds
— The personal data has been unlawfully processed (I did not consent
  to its collection)

Per Article 17(2), where you have made the personal data public, I
request that you inform all recipients of this erasure request.

If your search identifies multiple entries that could reasonably
pertain to me — including partial matches, variant spellings, or
records linked to different identifiers — I exercise my right of
access (Article 15) to review those entries so I can confirm that
every record pertaining to me is erased. Do not unilaterally
determine which records are or are not mine without providing me
the opportunity to verify.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
3. RIGHT TO OBJECT (Article 21)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

I object to ALL processing of my personal data, including but not
limited to processing for:
— Direct marketing (Article 21(2)) — this objection is absolute
— Profiling related to direct marketing (Article 21(2))
— Any processing based on legitimate interests (Article 6(1)(f))

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
4. EXPLICIT WITHDRAWAL OF CONSENT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Per Article 7(3), I hereby withdraw any and all consent — express
or implied — for the processing of my personal data by your
organization, for any purpose, now and in the future.

I DO NOT CONSENT to any future collection, processing, storage,
transmission, sale, licensing, or disclosure of my personal data
by your organization, from any source, for any purpose.

This withdrawal is documented and timestamped. Any subsequent
processing will occur without a valid legal basis under Article 6
and may constitute a violation subject to administrative fines
of up to €20,000,000 or 4% of annual worldwide turnover
(Article 83(5)).

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
5. REQUEST FOR DOCUMENTATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Upon completion of erasure, I request written confirmation including:
— The specific categories of data that were erased
— The date erasure was completed
— A list of ALL third parties who were notified of the erasure
— Confirmation that automated systems have been updated to
  prevent future re-collection of my data

Identifying Information:
Full Name: {full_name}
Email: {email}
Address: {address}

You are required to respond within one calendar month per
Article 12(3). This request is made under all privacy laws
applicable to your organization based on your place of
establishment and operations — in addition to those applicable
based on my residence. If your organization does business in
California or other US states with privacy laws, those
obligations apply concurrently with the GDPR.

This communication is archived for potential submission to the
relevant supervisory authority.

{full_name}
Date: {date}

us_omnibus.name: US Omnibus — All Applicable State Laws
us_omnibus.subject: Personal Data Deletion Request and Withdrawal of Consent — {full_name}
us_omnibus.body:
To the Privacy Officer:

I am writing to exercise my privacy rights under all applicable
federal and state laws, including but not limited to:

— California Consumer Privacy Act / California Privacy Rights Act
  (Cal. Civ. Code §§ 1798.100–1798.199.100)
— Virginia Consumer Data Protection Act (Va. Code §§ 59.1-575–59.1-585)
— Colorado Privacy Act (C.R.S. §§ 6-1-1301–6-1-1313)
— Connecticut Data Privacy Act (Conn. Gen. Stat. §§ 42-515–42-525)
— Utah Consumer Privacy Act (Utah Code §§ 13-61-101–13-61-404)
— Iowa Consumer Data Protection Act (Iowa Code ch. 715D)
— Indiana Consumer Data Protection Act (Ind. Code §§ 24-15-1–24-15-9)
— Tennessee Information Protection Act (Tenn. Code §§ 47-18-3201–3213)
— Montana Consumer Data Privacy Act (Mont. Code §§ 30-14-2801–2817)
— Texas Data Privacy and Security Act (Tex. Bus. & Com. Code ch. 541)
— Oregon Consumer Privacy Act (ORS §§ 646A.570–646A.604)
— Delaware Personal Data Privacy Act (Del. Code tit. 6, ch. 12D)
— New Jersey Data Privacy Act (N.J.S.A. §§ 56:8-166–56:8-181)
— New Hampshire Privacy Act (RSA 507-H)
— Nebraska Data Privacy Act (Neb. Rev. Stat. §§ 87-1101–87-1116)
— Minnesota Consumer Data Privacy Act (Minn. Stat. ch. 325O)
— Maryland Online Data Privacy Act (Md. Code, Com. Law §§ 14-4601–4614)
— Any other state, federal, or local privacy legislation that may apply

I hereby:

1. REQUEST DELETION of all personal information you hold about me
2. REQUEST DISCLOSURE of what data you held, where you obtained it,
   and to whom you have disclosed it
3. OPT OUT of the sale and sharing of my personal information
4. WITHDRAW ALL CONSENT for future collection, processing, storage,
   sale, sharing, or any other use of my personal information

I EXPLICITLY DO NOT CONSENT — now or in the future — to your
organization collecting, purchasing, licensing, scraping, inferring,
or otherwise obtaining my personal information from any source.
I do not consent to your organization creating, maintaining, or
updating any record, profile, or database entry about me.

This objection is unconditional and irrevocable. It applies to data
obtained from any source including but not limited to public records,
commercial data providers, web scraping, social media, and any other
means. The absence of current legal enforceability for this objection
in certain jurisdictions does not diminish my clearly expressed intent.

If your search identifies multiple entries that could reasonably
pertain to me — including partial matches, variant spellings, or
records linked to different addresses or identifiers — I exercise
my right to access and review those entries so I can confirm that
every record pertaining to me is deleted. Do not unilaterally
determine which records are or are not mine without providing me
the opportunity to verify.

Identifying Information:
Full Name: {full_name}
Email: {email}
Phone: {phone}
Address: {address}

This request is made under all privacy laws applicable to your
organization based on your place of incorporation, headquarters,
registration, and operations — in addition to those applicable
based on my residence. If your organization is established in or
does business in the European Union or United Kingdom, the GDPR
and UK GDPR apply concurrently. If your organization is
established in or does business in California, the California
Delete Act's registration and deletion requirements apply,
including $200 per day penalties for unfulfilled requests.

Please confirm deletion and disclosure in writing within 30 days.
This communication is timestamped and archived.

{full_name}
Date: {date}

noncompliance_notice.name: Non-Compliance Notice
noncompliance_notice.applicable_when: Broker failed to respond within legal deadline
noncompliance_notice.subject: SECOND NOTICE — Failure to Comply with Data Deletion Request — {full_name}
noncompliance_notice.body:
To the Privacy Officer:

On {original_request_date}, I submitted a legally valid data deletion
request to your organization regarding my personal information. As of
today's date, {days_elapsed} days have elapsed.

Under applicable law, you were required to respond within
{legal_deadline_days} days. You have failed to do so.

I am documenting this non-compliance. Please be advised:

1. I am filing a complaint with the relevant regulatory authority:
   — California Privacy Protection Agency (if CCPA applies)
   — Relevant state Attorney General
   — Federal Trade Commission
   — Information Commissioner's Office (if GDPR/UK GDPR applies)
   — Relevant EU supervisory authority

2. My original request remains in full force. I continue to demand:
   — Complete deletion of all personal information
   — Disclosure of data held and third parties it was shared with
   — Written confirmation of deletion

3. All previous consent withdrawals and objections remain in effect.

4. Continued processing of my personal information after receiving my
   deletion request and failing to comply constitutes willful violation
   and may result in enhanced penalties.

This is a final notice before regulatory complaint.

Original Request Date: {original_request_date}
Days Elapsed: {days_elapsed}
Reference: {request_reference_id}

{full_name}
Date: {date}

relisting_challenge.name: Re-listing Challenge — Documented Willful Violation
relisting_challenge.applicable_when: Broker re-listed data after previous deletion
relisting_challenge.subject: NOTICE OF WILLFUL RE-LISTING OF PERSONAL DATA — {full_name} — Deletion #{relist_count}
relisting_challenge.body:
To the Privacy Officer and Legal Department:

I have confirmed that your organization has RE-LISTED my personal
information after a previous deletion that was confirmed/completed
on {previous_deletion_date}.

This is re-listing occurrence #{relist_count}.

Documented history:
{deletion_history}

This pattern of re-listing personal information after receiving and
processing deletion requests raises serious concerns regarding:

1. WILLFUL NON-COMPLIANCE: Repeatedly re-acquiring and publishing
   personal information belonging to an individual who has explicitly
   requested deletion and withdrawn consent suggests intentional
   disregard for privacy rights.

2. ENHANCED PENALTIES: Under CCPA, intentional violations carry
   penalties of up to $7,500 per violation. Each re-listing after
   a deletion request may constitute a separate intentional violation.

3. ABSENCE OF CONSENT: As documented in my previous communications
   (dates: {previous_request_dates}), I have EXPLICITLY WITHDRAWN
   ALL CONSENT for collection, processing, and publication of my
   personal information. Your organization has re-collected and
   re-published my data AGAINST my documented objection.

I AGAIN demand:
— Immediate deletion of all personal information
— Disclosure of the source from which you re-acquired my data
— Implementation of technical measures to prevent future re-listing
— Written confirmation that suppression controls are in place

I am documenting this re-listing with screenshots and archived web
pages as evidence. This record is being maintained for potential
regulatory complaint and/or legal action.

{full_name}
Date: {date}

preemptive_blanket.name: Pre-emptive Blanket Objection
preemptive_blanket.applicable_when: Always — sent regardless of whether presence is confirmed
preemptive_blanket.subject: Data Deletion Request, Opt-Out, and Pre-emptive Objection to Processing — {full_name}
preemptive_blanket.body:
To the Privacy Officer:

Whether or not your organization currently holds my personal
information, I am writing to:

1. REQUEST DELETION of any personal information you currently hold
   about me under all applicable privacy laws.

2. OPT OUT of the sale and sharing of my personal information under
   CCPA § 1798.120 and equivalent state provisions.

3. OBJECT to all processing of my personal information under GDPR
   Article 21 (where applicable).

4. WITHDRAW ALL CONSENT — express or implied, past, present, or
   future — for your organization to collect, process, store, sell,
   share, or otherwise use my personal information, from any source,
   for any purpose.

5. PRE-EMPTIVELY OBJECT to any future acquisition, collection,
   purchase, licensing, scraping, or inference of my personal
   information by your organization.

I understand that this pre-emptive objection may not carry legal
force in all jurisdictions at this time. I am establishing a clear,
timestamped record of my wishes. This record may become legally
relevant as privacy legislation continues to evolve.

Your organization bears the burden of conducting a reasonable
search of all systems, databases, and records — including data
obtained from third parties — to identify any personal information
associated with the identifiers provided below. A claim that a
record pertains to a different individual of the same name does
not satisfy this obligation; under CCPA § 1798.105, GDPR Article
17, and equivalent state privacy laws, you must either delete the
matching records or provide a verifiable, documented basis for
your determination that they do not belong to me.

If your search identifies multiple entries that could reasonably
pertain to me — including partial matches, variant spellings,
or records linked to different addresses or identifiers — I
exercise my right of access (CCPA § 1798.110, GDPR Article 15)
to review those entries so I can confirm that every record
pertaining to me is deleted. Do not unilaterally determine which
records are or are not mine without providing me the opportunity
to verify.

I require the following responses in writing to {email}:

(a) ACKNOWLEDGE receipt of this request within 10 business days,
    as required by CCPA regulations (Cal. Code Regs. tit. 11,
    § 7023).

(b) DISCLOSE what personal information you hold about me and the
    categories of sources from which it was obtained, prior to
    deletion, as required under CCPA § 1798.110 and GDPR Article
    15.

(c) CONFIRM in writing when deletion is complete, specifying what
    data was deleted and from which systems, as required under
    GDPR Article 12(3) and CCPA regulations.

(d) NOTIFY all third parties to whom you have disclosed, sold, or
    shared my personal information to delete it as well, and
    provide me with a list of those third parties, per GDPR
    Article 19 and CCPA § 1798.105(c).

(e) CEASE any future collection, purchase, licensing, or inference
    of my personal information, including through your service
    providers and contractors, on an ongoing basis.

If you currently hold no data about me, I request written
confirmation of that fact and ask that this objection be recorded
in your systems to prevent future collection.

Response deadlines:
- CCPA/CPRA: 45 calendar days from receipt (Cal. Civ. Code
  § 1798.105).
- GDPR: 30 calendar days from receipt (GDPR Article 12(3)).
- California Delete Act: $200 per day per unfulfilled request
  (Cal. Civ. Code § 1798.99.86).

Failure to comply within the applicable deadline will be reported
to the relevant enforcement authority, including the California
Privacy Protection Agency (CPPA), the Federal Trade Commission
(FTC), and/or the appropriate EU/UK Data Protection Authority.

This request is submitted via email. Under CCPA § 1798.105(a),
GDPR Articles 12 and 17, and equivalent state privacy laws, you
are required to accept verifiable consumer requests submitted
through any reasonable means, including email. You may not
require me to use a specific web form, create an account, or
use any proprietary portal as a precondition for processing
this request.

This request is made under all privacy laws applicable to your
organization based on your place of incorporation, headquarters,
registration, and operations — in addition to those applicable
based on my residence. If your organization is established in
or does business in the European Union, the GDPR applies to all
personal data you process regardless of the data subject's
location (GDPR Article 3(1)). If your organization is
established in or does business in California, the CCPA applies
to all California residents' data and subjects you to the
California Delete Act's registration and deletion requirements.
Similar obligations may arise under the privacy laws of every
state and country in which you operate.

Identifying Information:
Full Name: {full_name}
Email: {email}
{additional_emails}

This communication constitutes a verifiable consumer request.
It is archived with timestamp verification and will be used
as evidence in any regulatory complaint or legal proceeding.

{full_name}
Date: {date}
